name: Community Legal Services Of Mid-Florida, Inc. - Tavares Office
description_short: Community Legal Services of Mid-Florida provides no-cost legal assistance across family law, housing, financial matters, and consumer rights to low-income residents through multiple office locations and virtual clinics.
description_long:
Community Legal Services (CLS) has been operating for 60 years as a non-profit legal aid organization serving residents of Central Florida. The organization is dedicated to "enhancing the lives of those we serve through legal support, assistance, and education," operating under the brand "Legal Access for All." Their mission centers on providing legal services to those who cannot afford private attorneys, with a particular focus on vulnerable populations including families, older adults, and low-income individuals facing housing or financial crises.

CLS offers a comprehensive range of legal services spanning multiple practice areas. These include family law matters (divorce, custody), housing and fair housing issues, financial matters and debt-related questions, disaster relief assistance, SNAP food assistance navigation, small business and non-profit formation, student and children's rights, violence protection orders, and services for older adults. The organization delivers services through multiple channels: in-person legal clinics at various office locations (including Tavares), virtual online clinics, and educational resources accessible through their website.

What distinguishes CLS is its commitment to accessibility through multiple delivery methods and geographic coverage. The organization hosts scheduled legal clinic events throughout their service area, including specialized clinics for family law and bankruptcy matters. Their virtual clinic offerings remove geographic barriers, allowing clients statewide or beyond to access legal guidance online. The 60-year operational history suggests institutional stability and deep community roots in Central Florida.

However, prospective clients should understand that CLS operates as a legal aid organization with capacity constraints typical of non-profits. Services are provided at no cost, which reflects their mission but also means appointment availability may be limited and wait times possible. The organization focuses on education and assistance rather than full legal representation in all cases. This is best suited for low-to-moderate income individuals who qualify for free legal aid services and need guidance on family law, housing, financial, or consumer protection matters.
pros: Completely free legal services and assistance for eligible low-income clients
60-year track record as established non-profit legal aid provider in Central Florida
Multiple office locations including Tavares office for geographic accessibility
Virtual and online clinic options eliminating travel barriers to legal help
Wide range of practice areas covering family law, housing, financial, and consumer protection matters
Specialized legal clinics for bankruptcy and family law with scheduled monthly offerings
Educational resources and legal information available online through their website
cons: Limited availability and likely appointment wait times due to non-profit capacity constraints
Services focus on legal guidance and assistance rather than guaranteed full representation
Income and eligibility requirements restrict access to low-income qualifying clients only
Geographic service area limited to Mid-Florida region (not statewide coverage except virtual clinics)
Non-profit staffing levels may result in longer processing times compared to private attorneys
best_for: Low-income families dealing with divorce, custody disputes, or child support matters
Renters and homeowners facing housing discrimination, eviction, or fair housing violations
Individuals seeking bankruptcy guidance and debt-related legal assistance
Older adults and vulnerable populations needing legal protection or advocacy
diagnosis: Community Legal Services is best for low-income Central Florida residents who qualify for free legal aid and need assistance with family law, housing, financial, or consumer protection matters. The primary caveat is that services are capacity-limited as a non-profit organization, eligibility requirements apply, and availability may require scheduling in advance or waiting for appointments.
services: Family law services (divorce, child custody, support matters)
Housing law and fair housing discrimination assistance
Financial matters and debt-related legal guidance
Bankruptcy legal clinics and filing support
SNAP food assistance application and benefits navigation
Small business and non-profit formation services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
